Title: Soil conditions and the plant microbiome boost the accumulation of monoterpenes in the fruit of Citrus reticulata ‘Chachi’

Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: bioactive essential oil components contributing to medicinal material/fruit quality
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes accumulation of multiple monoterpenes in C. reticulata ‘Chachi’, with Streptomyces inoculation significantly increasing α-pinene, β-pinene, α-thujene and β-myrcene in leaves.
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Synthetic community inoculation; GC-MS metabolite profiling; host leaf RNA-seq; qRT-PCR of DXS
Common_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: bacterial strain; contains terpene biosynthetic gene clusters and complete terpenoid backbone pathway
Common_Genes.Omics_Data: metagenomics; host RNA-seq; strain genome sequencing; metabolomics (GC-MS); CNSA accession CNP0002028
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Candidate beneficial microbe for precision microbiome management to enhance citrus monoterpene quality traits.
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Identified as the major rhizosphere-derived bacterial strain promoting monoterpene accumulation; effect strengthened when combined with Serra-11.
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Streptomyces strain Strep-4 significantly increased α-pinene, β-pinene, α-thujene and β-myrcene in citrus leaves. 2. Strep-4 treatment elevated host DXS expression. 3. Strep-4 and its SynCom induced host terpene-related and immune-related genes, supporting microbe-mediated enhancement of monoterpene accumulation.

Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: bioactive essential oil components contributing to medicinal material/fruit quality
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: leaf (SynCom assay); root endosphere potential contribution
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Enhances the monoterpene-promoting effect of Streptomyces Strep-4; as an endophyte, has potential to supply monoterpene precursors via a complete terpenoid backbone pathway.
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: SynCom inoculation; GC-MS metabolite profiling; bacterial genome sequencing and annotation
Common_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: bacterial strain; endophyte; complete terpenoid backbone pathway
Common_Genes.Omics_Data: endophyte 16S amplicon sequencing; bacterial genome sequencing; metabolomics (GC-MS); CNSA accession CNP0002028
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential endophytic inoculant to combine with beneficial rhizosphere microbes for improving citrus monoterpene quality.
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: The combination of Strep-4 and Serra-11 gave the highest α-pinene, β-pinene and α-thujene levels among tested treatments.
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Serra-11 alone was not reported to significantly raise leaf monoterpenes versus control. 2. Combined with Strep-4, Serra-11 strengthened monoterpene accumulation. 3. Genome analysis showed a complete terpenoid backbone pathway, supporting a possible role in supplying monoterpene intermediates in roots.

Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: plastidial methylerythritol phosphate pathway supplying monoterpene precursors
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Condenses pyruvate and glyceraldehyde 3-phosphate to form 1-deoxy-D-xylulose 5-phosphate, initiating the plastidial terpenoid precursor pathway.
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite: α-pinene; β-pinene; α-thujene; α-terpinene; β-myrcene; δ-carene; o-cymene
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: bioactive essential oil components determining medicinal quality
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Higher DXS expression is associated with increased monoterpene accumulation in core-region citrus and after Streptomyces/SynCom inoculation.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: RNA-seq; qRT-PCR; correlation analysis with monoterpene levels; SynCom response assay
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Thought to catalyze a critical step; treated in the paper as a key enzyme in terpene backbone biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Highly expressed in leaves and peel from core region; expression in leaves and roots higher than in peels; induced in leaves by Streptomyces and its SynCom
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: host RNA-seq; qRT-PCR; metabolomics (GC-MS); CNSA accession CNP0002028
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: core-region soil with high salinity, Mg, Mn and K; Streptomyces/SynCom inoculation
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Promising engineering target for boosting citrus monoterpenes, potentially combined with microbiome management and favorable mineral nutrition.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: The paper emphasizes DXS rather than monoterpene synthase genes because β-pinene synthase showed no regional differential expression; DXS was also present in bacterial genomes, but the core host-gene evidence concerns citrus DXS.
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. DXS was highly expressed in core-region citrus tissues, especially leaves and roots. 2. DXS expression pattern was confirmed by qRT-PCR. 3. Streptomyces and its SynCom significantly increased host DXS expression, linking precursor supply to higher monoterpene accumulation.

Pathway_Genes.Gene_Accession_Number: Q8L5K2 (reference sequence used for identification from Citrus medica var. limon)
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Cyclizes geranyl diphosphate to produce β-pinene.
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: bioactive essential oil component contributing to medicinal quality
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Detected in peel transcriptome, but showed no expression difference between core and non-core regions; therefore not supported as the main driver of regional monoterpene differences.
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Detected in peel samples; no differential expression between regions
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Less decisive than DXS in this study for explaining regional monoterpene accumulation.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Included because it is the only monoterpene synthase explicitly identified in the transcriptome, but the paper does not support it as a differential core determinant.
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. β-pinene synthase transcripts were found in peel samples. 2. No significant difference was observed between core and non-core regions. 3. The study therefore shifted emphasis to DXS as the more relevant pathway gene.

Regulation_Genes.Regulation_Mode: predicted transcriptional activation of terpene pathway gene expression
Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: monoterpene biosynthesis via regulation of precursor-supply gene expression and defense-associated signaling
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Predicted to bind the DXS promoter and regulate DXS expression
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite: α-pinene; α-thujene; β-myrcene; α-terpinene; β-pinene; δ-carene
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: bioactive essential oil components contributing to medicinal quality
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Positively associated with monoterpene accumulation; proposed to regulate DXS and thereby promote monoterpene biosynthesis under core-region conditions.
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Correlation analysis; transcriptomics; in silico promoter-binding prediction (JASPAR)
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Condition-associated positive regulator proposed to connect immune signaling with monoterpene biosynthesis
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. WRKY29 was positively associated with monoterpene-rich core-region samples. 2. JASPAR analysis predicted WRKY29 binding to the DXS promoter. 3. The study proposes WRKY29 as an immune-linked regulator of monoterpene biosynthesis.
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Regulation is prediction-based in this paper; no direct binding assay was performed.
Regulation_Genes.Upstream_Signals_or_Inputs: biotic stress; innate immune signaling; regional microbiome differences
Regulation_Genes.Omics_Data: host RNA-seq; promoter motif prediction; CNSA accession CNP0002028
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential regulatory target to couple immune activation with monoterpene enhancement, but requires direct validation.
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Detected among disease-resistance-associated genes in core-region samples
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Higher/associated in core-region tissues; positively correlated with monoterpenes
Regulation_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: core-region field conditions; microbiome-associated immune activation

Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: microbiome-associated activation of terpene synthesis through host immune signaling
Regulation_Genes.Primary_Regulatory_Targets: immune signaling module; downstream defense-associated terpene biosynthesis responses
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Associated with activation of defense and terpene-related gene expression
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: bioactive essential oil components contributing to medicinal quality
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Positive association with monoterpene accumulation and induction after beneficial bacterial inoculation, supporting immune-dependent terpene enhancement.
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Correlation analysis; RNA-seq after bacterial inoculation
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Immune-linked positive controller suggested to mediate microbiome effects on monoterpene accumulation
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. FLS2 expression positively correlated with monoterpene levels in field samples. 2. FLS2 was among immune genes induced after Strep-4/SynCom inoculation. 3. The paper proposes that rhizosphere microbes promote monoterpene accumulation through host immune interactions involving genes such as FLS2.
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Association-based; no genetic perturbation of FLS2 was conducted.
Regulation_Genes.Omics_Data: host RNA-seq; correlation network; CNSA accession CNP0002028
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential marker for selecting immune-responsive host–microbiome combinations that enhance monoterpenes.
Regulation_Genes.Protein_Complex_Involvement: receptor signaling complex not directly tested
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Positively correlated with monoterpenes; induced in leaves by Strep-4/SynCom

Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: salt/drought-responsive signaling associated with monoterpene accumulation
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: bioactive essential oil components contributing to medicinal quality
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Leaf PYR/PYL expression was positively correlated with several monoterpenes, implicating ABA/salt-stress signaling in enhanced monoterpene accumulation.
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Correlation analysis in transcriptome and environmental data
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Condition-dependent abiotic-stress signaling regulator linked to monoterpene-rich core-region soils
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. PYR/PYL transcripts were positively correlated with α-pinene, α-thujene, β-myrcene and α-terpinene in leaves. 2. PYR/PYL was also correlated with soil salinity and mineral factors. 3. The study links ABA receptor-mediated stress signaling with monoterpene accumulation under core-region soil conditions.
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Evidence is correlative rather than causal genetic validation.
Regulation_Genes.Upstream_Signals_or_Inputs: abscisic acid; salt stress; drought-related cues
Regulation_Genes.Omics_Data: host RNA-seq; environmental correlation analysis; CNSA accession CNP0002028
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: May serve as a marker for selecting stress-adapted genotypes or agronomic regimes favorable for monoterpene accumulation.
Regulation_Genes.Protein_Complex_Involvement: ABA receptor complex not directly tested
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Leaf expression positively correlated with multiple monoterpenes

Regulation_Genes.Regulation_Mode: conversion of salicylic acid to methyl salicylate to promote systemic acquired resistance signaling
Regulation_Genes.Primary_Regulatory_Targets: salicylic acid pool; systemic acquired resistance signaling
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: bioactive essential oil components contributing to medicinal quality
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Induced by Streptomyces/SynCom treatment and proposed to contribute to immune-dependent promotion of monoterpene accumulation.
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Supports the paper’s immune-dependent mechanism linking beneficial microbes to terpene accumulation
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. SAMT was highly expressed in leaves after Strep-4 and SynCom inoculation. 2. SAMT converts salicylic acid to methyl salicylate, a key SAR signal. 3. The study uses SAMT induction as evidence that beneficial bacteria activate immune pathways associated with higher monoterpenes.
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Not genetically validated as a direct regulator of terpene genes in this paper.
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential marker for monitoring microbe-induced immune states favorable for quality metabolite accumulation.
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Highly expressed in leaves after Strep-4 and SynCom treatments
